VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - Millet battaniyeyle yatıyor Sayın Ök, kalın giyiyorlar.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Şimdi, bugünkü değerlere baktığımızda, 2002 yılında, bakın, asgari ücretin 200 kw'ye kadar kullanılan elektrik için yüzde 20'si, doğal gaz için yüzde 25'i ödeniyordu yani ikisini topladığımızda yüzde 45'e tekabül ediyor. Yani 22.104 lira asgari ücretin diyelim 10 bin lirası doğal gaz ile elektrik kullanma gidiyordu. Bugün geldiğimizde, Ekim 2025 verilerine baktığımızda, asgari ücretteki payının meskenlerde elektrik için yüzde 2,3; doğal gaz için ise yüzde 5,8 olduğunu görüyoruz; topladığımızda bu da yüzde 8'e tekabül ediyor. Aşağı yukarı topladığımızda yani hesapladığımızda, asgari ücrete vurduğumuzda 1.700-1.800 lira yani 11 bin lira mı, 1.800 lira mı?
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Yani asgari ücret gayet iyi seviyede mi diyorsunuz? Asgari ücret yeterli mi diyorsunuz?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Devlet eliyle baktığımızda, bugün normal standart bir ailenin 1.800 lira ödediğini, bundan yirmi yıl önce de 11 bin yani bu bir iyileşme değil midir?
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - O zaman siz asgari ücretle geçinin.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Bu, vatandaşımızın menfaatine bir düzeltme değil midir? Bunu sormak istiyorum.
Bunun haricinde, ihtiyaç sahibi olan vatandaşlarımızı, tüketimi 150 kw'ye kadar olan vatandaşlarımızı da Aile ve Sosyal Politikalar Bakanlığımız bünyesinden destekliyoruz.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Sayın Bakanım, Bakanlığınız bütçesinin hayırlı, uğurlu ve bereketli olmasını temenni ediyorum.
Teşekkürler.
records.englishTranslation:
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Thank you, Mr. President.
Mr. President, Mr. Minister, our esteemed Deputy Ministers, esteemed members of our Commission, esteemed bureaucrats and members of the press; I greet you all with respect and love.
First of all, Mr. Minister, thank you very much for your detailed presentation. In fact, when we look at the overall presentation, you have presented not only the budget but also the revolutionary steps Türkiye has taken in energy in such a summary - perspective. I thank you very much. I would also like to express my gratitude to our Ministry for its foresight on this issue and for providing a forward-looking perspective not only today but also in the next thirty and fifty years.
As we all know, we are a net exporter of energy. As you know, we have implemented our national energy and mining policy with the aim of becoming fully independent in the field of energy. This policy is in the form of a road map that aims to reduce or even completely end our foreign dependency by revealing our energy and natural resource potential.
The basis of our energy policies is the goal of providing uninterrupted, high-quality and sustainable energy to our citizens. We are taking determined steps to avoid being affected by price fluctuations and supply risks that may occur in international markets. In this context, we continue to take important steps to provide new resource and route diversity, increase our domestic and national production, and strengthen supply security. Turkey is now - Mr. Minister, as you said in your presentation, it is one of the parts that I care about the most - and is taking firm steps towards becoming not only a consumer country in energy but also an energy base in its region; It is actually in the form of an energy "hub". Therefore, yes, we will use the energy we produce first for our needs, but I also think it is important that we use and sell it for the benefit of our nation in our region as needed.
Of course, here we are talking about the morning. Of course, the opposition will oppose, that is, regarding the budget, energy policies, but we need to look at it this way: No HEPP, no RES, no SPP; nuclear, yes, not nuclear, okay; mine, in underground mines. So, what will happen? Here you go. Let's imagine that there is a power outage for one night, one day, twenty-four hours. When we really look at it, we would probably rebel from the very beginning. You mostly use phones, what do we do with phones? We charge, simply put. From the refrigerator etc. Therefore, I think that in order to ensure continuity in energy, we need to do whatever is necessary, both above and below ground, in terms of mines, and also in terms of energy. Because that's what the world does. The world even does something that if it is not available in its own country, it goes to other countries. We do this too, we do this too. Our esteemed Minister said in his presentations today; Again, he talked about the work done in Somalia and Hungary. I congratulate you in this sense. I would like to say once again that I am proud of my country in this regard.

DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Let's talk about the Eastern Mediterranean.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Of course I will tell you, I will get to that too.
When we look at 2002, our Minister immediately put it in perspective, but I want to go over it once again. Now, 40 percent of our primary energy supply was oil, 19 percent was natural gas, while the share of renewable sources was 5 percent; Today, at this rate, the share of renewable resources is 15 percent, the share of natural gas is 27 percent, the share of coal - which remains the same - is 25 percent, and the share of oil varies from 40 percent to 30 percent. Again, 63 percent of the electricity we produced in 2024 was provided by renewable energy sources, 34.7 percent from coal, and 19 percent from natural gas, meaning the total share of domestic and renewable resources reached 61 percent. Our renewable electricity production, which was 34 billion kilowatt-hours in 2002, increased by 4.8 times to 164 billion kilowatt-hours in 2024. Likewise, our electricity installed power, which was 31,846 megawatts, has increased fourfold in the last twenty-three years, reaching 121,418 megawatts today. Again, our installed power from renewable energy, which was 12,305 megawatts in 2002, has reached 74,746 megawatts as of September 2025. Almost 2 thirds, or 61.6 percent, of our electricity installed capacity
consists of renewable energy sources. At the end of August 2025, the ratio of the installed power of production facilities based on renewable resources to the total installed power reached 61.4 percent. The ratio of the installed power of production facilities based on domestic and renewable resources to the total installed power has reached 71 percent. With our goal of national power in production, we are among the European leaders in installed capacity. Look, only 387 wind power plants, 36 thousand solar power plants, 771 hydroelectric plants, 66 geothermal power plants, and 403 biomass plants contribute to production. The number of power plants has increased from around 300 to over 38 thousand today. Each of them is the voice of local resources, each of them is a source of power rising from all over Anatolia. Today, Türkiye ranks 5th in Europe and 11th in the world in terms of renewable energy installed capacity; It ranks 7th in Europe and 13th in the world in terms of wind installed capacity; It ranks 6th in Europe and 13th in the world in solar energy installed capacity; It ranks 2nd in Europe and 8th in the world in terms of HEPP installed capacity; It ranks 1st in Europe and 8th in the world in geothermal energy. You know, "What are you doing?" I wanted to remind these again for those who say:
Again, according to 2024 data, the share of coal in electricity production in our country is 34.7 percent, 13.6 of which is met by domestic coal. We know very well that domestic coal is the guarantee of our energy supply security and its importance. All these investments are actually indicators of the most important steps we have taken towards Türkiye's achieving a strong and independent structure based on domestic energy resources.
When we look at the electrical energy infrastructure, the length of the electricity transmission line has been increased from 42 thousand kilometers to 76 thousand kilometers, and the length of the distribution line has been increased from 781 thousand kilometers to 1.5 million kilometers.

Dear Minister, you said something like this in your presentation, which is very important: "Intermittent production of renewable energy resources requires a strong grid infrastructure." you say. Therefore, we are preparing our network consisting of 1.5 million kilometers of distribution lines for the future, and you mentioned that we are preparing it for the future with an investment of 30 billion dollars. I think this is very important because sometimes, when companies apply to connect to the lines to use the electricity they produce and need, they receive such rejection answers stating that the capacity is currently full or that the line cannot handle the required capacity. In this sense, I find this very true. I honestly think that the reason for those increases in transmission and distribution lines - my interpretation - is due to the investment we will make in this. At this point today, the number of electricity subscribers has reached 51 million from 25 million.
In 2002, when we started using natural gas, we consumed 17 billion cubic meters of natural gas. Today, this figure has exceeded 53 billion cubic meters by the end of 2024, meaning that our subscribers, which were only 1.5 million, have exceeded 22 million today. Of course, natural gas was supplied to 5 provinces, but today we have delivered natural gas to 948 settlements in 81 provinces. Of course, we do not see this just as an energy investment. This is a social transformation that improves the quality of life of our citizens. Most of all, in the villages and cities - natural gas has now reached the villages and districts - the best example is saving it from the stove buckets prepared by our aunts. Today, our population that can use natural gas has reached 85 percent - which is very important. In this sense, I congratulate our Ministry.
We rapidly continue our exploration and production activities not only in natural mines but also in oil and natural gas. Frankly, we said we could do this with our own ships, human resources and equipment, and we set out. We have created a very strong fleet, especially deep-sea drilling ships. Today, Fatih, Kanuni, Yavuz, Abdülhamit Han drilling ships; We have one of the world's leading and modern oil and natural gas exploration and production fleets with our Oruç Reis, Barbaros Hayrettin Paşa seismic research and Osmangazi floating production platform ships. Of course, at this time, all the way back to 2016 and 2018, it was always the same.
There was an argument. Their costs were questioned as to why we are buying these ships, why we are having them bought, but at the point we have reached today, again in these Plan and Budget negotiations, for the 2 new ships we are buying - we are buying 2 new 7th generation offshore drilling ships - we are asking again, "Why are we buying them?" "Was it necessary?" or "You saw a kebab there" about his income at work. Frankly, I don't think it's right to say that. I don't think it's right to always look for something underlying Türkiye's breakthrough or any innovation. I think sometimes it is necessary to congratulate.
Yes, could it be rented? It was rented. My seventh budget in Plan and Budget, seventh or eight; I was on the Planning and Budget Commission for eight years.

Our previous Minister, Berat Albayrak, also explained very clearly at that time that they should have their own drilling ships instead of renting them. It was clearly explained in previous budget discussions today.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - Ms. Nilgün, which Minister is better if we compare?
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - Both are good.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - The seat of the Minister of Energy is shaking. With the cabinet change, Akın Gürlek may replace the Minister of Energy.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - They are both very good. This is a relay race for us, as you know, for the AK PARTY. I will tell you from here; Berat Albayrak Fortunately, our Minister took these steps. If he had not taken these steps, we would be talking about very different things today. Imagine renting drilling ships for a month, come on, that's it. As you know, there may be problems with permits and you may face different diplomatic risks, but today, if you have your own drilling ships, you can use them correctly. Moreover, we cannot search, let's say seismic.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Ms. Nilgün should explain the daily cost of drilling ships, let the Minister explain.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Mr. Deniz, please. The Minister will explain it, but do not interfere with my speech.
President.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Look, I say crazy challenge, crazy challenge! Let me explain, how much are the daily costs?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Mr. Deniz, you can ask the Minister about this.
İSMAİL GÜNEŞ (Uşak) - Mr. Deniz, your performance is low this year.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - İsmail Bey.
I want to say this: Yes, we rented drilling ships, maybe we searched for them, but they were not found. We can rent these, that is, the ships we have purchased as a country and added to our fleet today can also be rented. Therefore, we should not look at these things as kebabs. It's a good thing we didn't look anyway.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Nobody says anything about drilling ships, but Mr. Minister should also explain their daily costs.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - You know, look, we have serious work on the storage of natural gas. As of today, we currently have a total storage capacity of 5.8 billion cubic meters in natural gas tanks. In fact, this also provides us with the following: It also shows that we can use or allow the use of our natural gas or energy whenever and however we want.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Storage capacity is very low.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Of course, likewise, there are very important steps we have taken regarding oil and pipelines. In addition to the discoveries we make in our seas, we are also making important discoveries on land. Our country's oil production is increasing day by day, especially in the wells that are commissioned one after another, especially in the Southeastern Anatolia Region. Today, with the discovery made in Gabar alone, 31 thousand barrels of daily production are made in 137 wells. We have reached 176 thousand barrels of crude oil production, 136 thousand inside the country and 40 thousand outside the country. Frankly, I think that this production is a historical threshold that Türkiye has reached on the path to energy independence.

We are in the phase of transforming from energy corridors to energy centres. Frankly, we set out on this path to make Türkiye not only an energy transition route but also a regional energy center. Lines such as Iraq-Türkiye, Baku-Tbilisi-Ceyhan, Iran-Türkiye, TANAP, TurkStream are indicators that we have actually opened a new page in the history of energy diplomacy. As you know, TANAP provided gas flow for the first time in 2018, and by connecting to TAP in 2020, we established a bridge carrying energy to Europe. Again, with TurkStream, we ended dependence on the western line. We have established a direct purchase system from Russia and Türkiye-Nakhchivan natural gas in 2025.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Will you be able to buy it?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - We can buy it, don't worry.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Trump "Do not buy." he says.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Don't worry.
Look, we saw what happened during the pandemic period. Türkiye really provided gas to Europe as a guarantee in this process. You, Mr. Deniz, don't worry.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - That's why it was reflected in the press.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Now that foreign diplomacy is yours. The world has changed a lot now. Sometimes the balances may change daily, monthly or due to different strategic partnerships.
I would also like to express this: We have been serving our nation in Türkiye for twenty-three years under the leadership of our President - since you mentioned it. While we serve our nation, we have always been responsible for Türkiye and our country.
We have never taken any steps to subjugate any country. In fact, if we had taken such a step, our nation would not have brought us to power, which is right and wrong.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Are you in the clear when you are elected?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - .but he is informing this to all of us, to the whole of Türkiye.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Is everyone elected cleared?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Therefore, you do not have that in the morning either, sir, with America falling under its legitimacy and so on.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Tell that to Tom Barrack.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - .as he expressed it.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Say "I condemn Tom Barrack." say.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - There is no such thing, you perceive it that way.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - No, tell me.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Look, your Özgür Özel also appeared - now, I was going to say precious metals when I came to that section - "Wow, sir, you made a deal with America." saying; where?
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Oh, tell me, "I condemn Tom Barrack." Say, "I condemn America." say.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Mr. Minister explains, explains in the press, and also explains on the official website of the Ministry that there is no such thing.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - Accept our bill, we have a bill.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - But unfortunately, there is such a thing before us.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Why can't you condemn Tom Barrack?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Someone should lie.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Why can't you condemn it now?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - "Let's confuse everyone." then continue.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - "Trump will give legitimacy to Tayyip Erdoğan." He said, why can't you condemn it?

NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Look at your own leader first.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Do you hear?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - You first.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Do you hear?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Didn't your previous leader leave?
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Why don't you condemn Tom Barrack?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Oh my God! After the economic coup in 2018.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - I repeat what Tom Barrack said, you are reacting to me.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Didn't Kılıçdaroğlu go to England in 2018, 2019, 2020?
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Deniz.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - "Don't come to Türkiye, Türkiye is not a country to invest in." he said.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - When Tom Barrack says.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - We talked before noon, but there is no such procedure.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - But he doesn't hear.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - It was entered into the minutes, if he responds he will, if he doesn't it's up to him.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - He does not condemn you for not doing what you say, otherwise we would condemn you.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - He toured Europe door to door, nothing changed, CHP is the same CHP. When today came, Özgür Özel was left begging for help from England.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - How far did you take the words to avoid condemning Tom Barrack?
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - We condemn Tom Barrack, we condemn England, we condemn those who want mandate protection, we also condemn you.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Please, us.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - You jump from topic to topic in order not to condemn Tom Barrack.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - President, please.
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - Condemn Tom Barrack, really condemn him.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Please, do not confuse us with yourself.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Do you know who Tom Barrack is?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Please, do not confuse us with yourself.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Oh, do you know him?
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - This is not a colonial state.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - If you allow, I would like to continue my speech. Now, yes.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Do you know who Donald Trump is? President of the United States.
İSMAİL GÜNEŞ (Uşak) - Mr. Deniz, there is no minister among you.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Do you condemn Tom Barrack or not?
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Deniz, please do not repeat this. It is not asked 10 times, you asked, it was recorded in the minutes; If he answers, he will, if not, what should I do?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Mr. President, dear members of parliament; Yes, we established Europe's first and only integrated solar panel factory within the scope of YEKA application. We commissioned Konya Karapınar SPP, the largest solar power plant in Europe, with the panels produced in this factory.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - I mean, he jumped from topic to topic, but somehow the topic couldn't reach Tom Barrack.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - Are we going to ask you what to do?
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - "I condemn." What will happen?

NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - In addition, as a result of our installation work, we have achieved domestic production capacity of 91 percent in solar panels and 65 percent in wind turbines in our equipment production; we are proud of it. You also said that we will open a YEKA competition with at least 2000 megawatts of domestic components every year until 2035. In this sense, I think this is also important.
Akkuyu Nuclear Power Plant; The first unit is expected to start production in 2026. It will have a total installed capacity of 4800 megawatts. When the power plant is commissioned, it will produce 35 billion kilowatt hours of energy annually.
Now, when we look at energy, there are more than 400 different nuclear power plants in the world today. Today, work on new nuclear energy investment plants continues in more than 15 countries, so we will continue too. We also see nuclear energy as clean energy, which we believe will make great environmental contributions to net zero carbon emissions in 2053.
Again, as you know, site approval was given for the Sinop Nuclear Power Plant. Preliminary permits and project implementation permits were again provided for the Thrace Nuclear Power Plant. Here the bride said, "Sir, he planted the flag of Russia." Mr. Agbaba said, "You planted the Russian flag." saying. It can be done; Partnerships can be realized with companies and countries as investments, experience transfers can be made, but you cannot do this by going from here and saying, "He planted the Russian flag in Mersin." Frankly, I do not think it is right to make such statements here in the morning, as if we have given away every inch of the country.
Yes, I have three minutes left.
AŞKIN GENÇ (Kayseri) - Fifty seconds.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Now, let's talk about the electricity and natural gas supports provided to our citizens. This has been said before, it was said last year. Today, our government subsidizes 57 percent of the low-end electricity used in homes, 36 percent of the high-end electricity, and 43 percent of the natural gas. This amount in 2025 alone is 694 billion TL.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - To whom did you pay that money? Who did you pay? To electricity distribution companies.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - With a new regulation, we are implementing gradual tariff management so that our citizens who consume less electricity pay less.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - This is not for you.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Payment guarantee has been given to electricity distribution companies.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - You are worthless.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - This is a payment guarantee.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Now, when we look, they said here the head of the household.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - The institutions that are getting richest right now are the electricity distribution companies.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - It is stated that there is an increase in the support given per household due to the transition to this gradual transition.
It was said last year and this year too.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - You know that the citizens cannot pay that bill, so you pay some of it to the companies.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Ms. Nilgün, I give two minutes of additional time.

NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - If a household uses up to 200 kilowatt-hours of electricity, we support it, but if it uses more than twice that, or over 400 kilowatt-hours, we do not support it. For example, in my house in Denizli, everything is electric, we heat and cool with electricity. The government should not support me because my electricity usage is too high and I have made my choice accordingly. The system we brought and implemented is exactly in this direction.
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - People use air conditioners, air conditioners.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - People sleep with blankets, Mr. Ök, they wear thick clothes.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Now, when we look at today's values, in 2002, 20 percent of the minimum wage was paid for electricity used up to 200 kW and 25 percent for natural gas, so when we add the two together, it corresponds to 45 percent. So, let's say 10 thousand lira of the 22,104 lira minimum wage was spent on natural gas and electricity usage. Today, when we look at the October 2025 data, the share of the minimum wage is 2.3 percent for electricity in residences; For natural gas, we see that it is 5.8 percent; When we add it up, this corresponds to 8 percent. When we add up more or less, that is, when we calculate, when we calculate the minimum wage, is it 1,700-1,800 liras, that is, 11 thousand liras or 1,800 liras?
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - So, are you saying that the minimum wage is at a very good level? Do you say minimum wage is enough?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - When we look at it from the state, today a normal standard family pays 1,800 lira, twenty years ago it was 11 thousand, so isn't this an improvement?
DENİZ YAVUZYILMAZ (Zonguldak) - Then you can live on minimum wage.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Isn't this a correction for the benefit of our citizens? I want to ask this.
Apart from this, we also support our citizens in need and those whose consumption is up to 150 kW through our Ministry of Family and Social Policies.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Dear Minister, I hope that the budget of your Ministry will be beneficial, auspicious and fruitful.
Thanks.

records.originalTurkish:
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Teşekkür ediyorum Sayın Başkan.
Çok Değerli Bakanım, çok Değerli Komisyonumuzun kıymetli üyeleri, Bakan Yardımcılarım, çok değerli basınımızın güzide temsilcileri; ben de hepinizi sevgi ve saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Maalesef, dün Gürcistan'da düşen uçağımızda vefat eden, şehit olan askerlerimize başsağlığı diliyorum. Mekânları cennet olsun. Ailelerimizin, vatanımızın başı sağ olsun diyorum.
Tabii, Sayın Bakanım, öncelikle sunumunuz için çok teşekkür ediyorum, gerçekten çok detaylı, her alana dokunulmaya çalışılmış. Aslında ülkemizin de en önemli Bakanlıklarından biri ve ülkenin hem en önemli Bakanlığı hem de en önemli hassas kesimlerine hizmet götüren bir Bakanlık. Bu anlamda da sizin şahsınızda tüm götürmüş olduğunuz çalışmalardan dolayı hepinizi tebrik ediyorum, teşekkürlerimi sunuyorum.
Tabii, biz diyoruz ki güçlü aile, güçlü Türkiye anlayışıyla, aile odaklı bir bakış açısıyla bugüne kadar, yirmi üç yıldır hizmet götürdük ama tabii ki güçlü aile derken güçlü kadını da çok büyük önemsiyoruz çünkü eğer kadın güçlü olmazsa aile güçlü olmaz, aile güçlü olmazsa da ülkenin güçlü olmayacağının bilincinin farkındayız. Evet, aslında biz kadın ve aile politikalarını birbirini tamamlayan ve destekleyen, el birliğiyle toplumun temellerini sağlamlaştıran 2 önemli politika olduğunu düşünüyoruz ve bu yoğun da politikalar üretiyoruz yani kadın hakları ile aile politikalarını hiçbir zaman birbirinin karşıtı olarak görmüyoruz, asla da görmedik, tam tersini bunu bir tamamlayıcı olarak görüyoruz, hem kadınların hem de ailenin güçlenmesi yönünde çok ciddi çalışmalar yapıyoruz.
Şimdi, kadın hakları denildiğinde akla ilk gelen kuşkusuz ki eğitimdir çünkü eğitim, kadınların ekonomik, sosyal ve kültürel haklarını kullanabilmesi için en önemli fırsat eşitliğidir. Kadınların eğitime erişimi engellendiğinde sadece onların geleceği değil, tüm toplumun kalkınma potansiyeli de engellenmiş olur. Geçmişte özgürce eğitime katılamayan kadınların eğitim ve iş gücü piyasasından da engellenerek sözde hani, o makbul kadın dayatmasıyla toplumsal ilerlemenin önüne aslında set çekilmişti ama biliyorsunuz, yapmış olduğumuz mücadelemizle eğitimdeki eşitsizlikleri ortadan kaldırdık, yasakları tarihe gömerek kadınların eğitimde daha fazla yer almasını sağlamak yönünde politikaları hayata geçirdik. Bunun sadece kadınlar için değil, aynı zamanda toplumun tümü için çok önemli bir kazanç olduğunu düşünüyoruz. Tabii ki kadının iş gücüne katılım oranları konusunda eleştiriler oldu ama işte o geçmişte kadınlar tam anlamıyla eğitim hayatlarına devam edebilseydi ya da iş hayatlarına o işte görünüşlerinden dolayı iş hayatlarında da aktif olarak daha fazla rol alabilselerdi belki bu oranlar çok daha farklı yerlerde olabilirdi. Ama bugün üniversite öğrencileri arasında kız öğrencilerimiz o eksikliği, farkı kapatmış durumda; yüzde 52'ye çıkmış durumda, yine, kadın hâkim oranına baktığımızda yüzde 47'lerde, yine kamudaki öğretmen oranına baktığımızda yüzde 60 civarında, kadın girişimci oranımız da yüzde 36'larda.
Biliyorsunuz, tabii, cumhuriyetin
ilk yıllarında önemli kazanım elde ettik Mustafa Kemal Atatürk sayesinde, Türk kadınının Parlamentoda seçme ve seçilme hakkıyla birlikte. Tabii o yıllarda oran yüzde 4 küsurlardaydı, sonraki yıllarda bu oran ciddi anlamda düşüyor ama 2002 yılına kadar hani o yüzde 4 eşiği aşılamamış durumdaydı ama çok şükür bugün geldiğimiz noktada evet çok iyi midir? Değildir ama 2002'den itibaren yüzde 4 kırılarak bugün yüzde 20'lere çıkmış durumda. Tabii, biz AK PARTİ olarak ve ilk hükûmetlerimiz zamanında seçim beyannamemizde de özellikle kadına yönelik şiddet, töre ve namus cinayetleriyle ilgili kadınlara çok önemli sözler vermiştik ve bugün bu sözleri yerine getirmiş olmanın da aslında bir mutluluğunu yaşıyoruz. 2004 yılında Anayasa değişikliğiyle "Kadın ve erkek eşittir." hükmünü Anayasa metnine dâhil ederek bu sözümüzü tuttuk.

Mücadele Direktifi'ni yayınlamış durumda ve bununla biz aslında Türkiye'yi böyle kıyasladığımızda ve 45 ülkenin imza koyduğu İstanbul Sözleşmesi'yle aslında direktif diyor ki: "Siz bu sözleşmeye imza koydunuz ve Avrupa Birliğindeki ülkeler maalesef, siz bununla ilgili gerekli düzenlemeleri yapmadınız." diyor yani diyor ki Avrupa Parlamentosu: "Bu direktifi tamamlamak için ve 51 maddelik direktifi, siz bunları tamamlayın, bunu tamamlamanız için de üç yıllık size süre tanınmıştır." Türkiye'ye baktığımızda söz konusu direktifteki maddelerin tamamıyla ilgili düzenlemeleri gerek 6284 sayılı Kanun'da gerekse Türk Ceza Kanunu'nu ve iç hukuki düzenlemeleri hayata geçirmiş tek ve örnek ülkedir ve sözleşmeye imza konulan 2011'den çok çok daha önce 2005 yılında yaptığımız Türk Ceza Kanunu düzenlemesiyle kadına yönelik şiddeti biraz önce de söylediğim gibi ilk defa suç olarak tanımladık.
Şimdi, İstanbul Sözleşmesi'nden çıkmış olmamız demek bir mücadeleden vazgeçmişiz anlamında değildir. Bu aslında, bugüne kadar yapılan çalışmaların görmezden gelinmesidir. Türkiye, gerçekten sözleşmeden çıktıktan sonra da bu yönde düzenlemeleri hayata geçirmeye devam etmiştir. "Bunlar nelerdir?" dersek, kadına yönelik şiddetin katalog suç hâline getirilmesi, boşanılmış eşe karşı işlenen suçun tıpkı nikâhlı eşe işlenmiş gibi ceza alması, iyi hâl indiriminin kaldırılması gibi düzenlemeler, daha sonra hayata geçirdiğimiz, hep beraber Mecliste hayata geçirdiğimiz düzenlemelerdir. Öyle ki direktifte dahi öngörülmeyen mağdurun kimliğinin ve adresinin değiştirilmesi, elektronik kelepçe uygulaması gibi tedbirleri de hâlihazırda ülkemiz uygulamaktadır.
Bugün 6284 sayılı Kanun, Türk Ceza Kanunu'ndaki değişiklikler, Anayasa düzenlemeleri, Medeni Kanun'daki eşitlik ilkesi ve en önemlisi ulusal eylem planlarımız sayesinde aslında bu mücadelede artık kurumsal değil veriye dayalı ve sürekli hâle geldiğimizin de göstergesidir. Evet, 2007 yılından bu yana düzenli olarak hazırlanan ulusal eylem planlarıyla artık bu alanda dönemsel değil stratejik bir mücadele yürütüyoruz. 81 ilde elektronik kelepçe uygulaması var ve yine yaklaşık günlük 1.200 elektronik kelepçe takılan suçlular takip edilmekte. Yine KADES, yine Alo 183 Şiddet Hattı gibi önemli düzenlemeleri hayata geçirdik.
Kadın konukevlerine gelince Eylül 2025 itibarıyla 150 kadın konukevimiz hizmet vermekte ve sadece Bakanlığımıza bağlı olanların sayısı 112; toplam kapasitesi de 2.819. Bugün 16 ilde de 19 konukevi mağdurların özel ihtiyaçlarına göre hizmet vermekte. Konukevlerinin sayısının azaltıldığıyla ilgili eleştirilerde bulunuldu. Hepimiz biliyoruz, nüfusu 100 binin üstünde olan belediyelerin konukevlerini açma zorunlulukları var. Şimdi, böyle bir şeye baktığımızda, belediyelere baktığımızda -ben İzmir'e baktım- İstanbul'a baktığımızda, İstanbul'da mesela Büyükşehir Belediyesi 1 tane kurmuş, 2014 yılında kurmuş, 2014 yılı AK PARTİ döneminde, ilave bir şey yok. Gelelim İzmir'e, İzmir'de 6 tane kadın konukevi var, bu 6 tanesinin 4 tanesi belediyeler tarafından kapatılmış durumda. Şimdi, baktığımızda da bununla da ilgili, bence, gidip bunlarla da ilgili onlara da sormak lazım belediyelerinize "Neden kapatıyorsunuz?" diye.
Sayın Bakanım, çok önemli bulduğum; biliyorsunuz, 2002 yılında kadına yönelik şiddete dair ulusal bir veri tabanı yoktu, bununla ilgili 2008 yılında yapılmıştı, sonra 2014 yılında, on sene önce, en son da işte 2024 ve 2025 dönemlerinde TÜİK'le yine Marmara Üniversitesinde ve diğer üniversitenin iş birliği içinde Türkiye'de Kadına Yönelik Şiddet Araştırması yapıldı. Açıkçası, araştırmanın geniş kapsamlı örnekleriyle ulusal temsiliyeti sağlayacak biçimde tasarlanmış olduğunu görüyoruz. Şimdi, son araştırma da şöyle; 18.275 kadınla yüz yüze yapılan görüşmelerde şu çıkmış: Kadınların yüzde 28,2'sinin yaşamı boyunca psikolojik, yüzde 18,3'ünün ekonomik, yüzde 12,8'inin fiziksel şiddete maruz kaldığı bu araştırma sonucunda belirlenmiş; yine, son on iki ayda ise kadınların yüzde 11,6'sının psikolojik, yüzde 3,7'sinin dijital, yüzde 3,2'sinin ekonomik, yüzde 3,1'inin ısrarlı takip, yüzde 2,6'sının fiziksel şiddete maruz kaldığı ortaya çıkmıştır. Tabii, şöyle baktığımızda, Dünya Sağlık Örgütünün de 2021 tarihli son yayınladığı "Kadına Yönelik Şiddet Yaygınlık Tahminleri" raporuna göre dünya ortalamasının yüzde 27 olduğunu görüyoruz yani kadına yönelik şiddet sadece Türkiye'nin değil tüm dünyanın ciddi anlamda mücadele etmesi gereken bir konudur.

ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - Benim derdim de tatmin olmak değil, siz tatmin oluyor musunuz, önemli olan o.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Yine, 2026 yılı bütçemizden ödeme gücü olmayan vatandaşlarımızın sağlık prim giderlerini karşılamak amacıyla, bakın,
157 milyar TL; 65 yaş üstü yaşlılarımız, bakıma ihtiyacı olan engelli vatandaşlarımız ile yakınlarına bağlanan aylıklar kapsamında 106 milyar TL. Bakın, Türkiye'nin ilk Engelliler Kanunu'nu 2005 yılında biz hayata geçirdik. Engellilerin kamuda daha fazla görünmesi için kotaları artırdık; yine, işveren prim desteklerini hayata geçirdik. Yine, sosyal konut finansmanının desteklenmesi amacıyla bu bütçeden 100 milyar lira ayırıyoruz. Yine, engelli vatandaşlarımızın evde bakım desteği amacıyla 90 milyar, engelli vatandaşlarımızın eğitim desteği için 56 milyar, doğum yardımı ödemeleri için 44 milyar, ekonomik yoksulluk için de çocuklarımızın ailelerinin yanında yetişmelerine imkân sağlayan sosyoekonomik destek ödemeleri için 23 milyar, koruyucu aile uygulamaları kapsamında 3 milyar TL kaynak ayırmış bulunmaktayız. Yine, elektrik üretim desteği kapsamında 3,5 milyon haneye 486 milyar, doğal gaz desteği için de 679 bin haneye 117 milyar TL ayırmış bulunmaktayız. Böylece "Ailenin Korunması ve Güçlendirilmesi" programına önceki yıla göre yüzde 30'un üzerinde, "Çocukların Korunması ve Gelişiminin Sağlanması" programına yüzde 34, "Kadının Güçlenmesi" programına da yüzde 35 artışla bu bütçede kaynak ayırmış bulunmaktayız.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - İki dakika ek süre.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - Yüzde 1,2.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Toparlıyorum Sayın Başkanım.
Biliyorum, sürem bitiyor ama çok önemli bulduğum bu özellikle çocukevleriyle ilgili, aile ortamında büyümesini çok önemsiyorum. Zaman zaman çocukevlerine ziyarette bulunuyorum, orada bulunan çocuklarımız sadece aile ortamında kalıyorlar, önceden koğuş tipi yerlerde kalıyorlardı. Artık çocuklarımız orada kalmakla sadece aile ortamı değil, her çocuğumuz ilgisi bulunan alanda resim, müzik, spor olmak üzere farklı alanlara gidiyorlar ve özel ders almaları sağlanıyor ve inanılmaz da mutlular, çocuklarımızın yüzlerinin güldüğünü görmek bizim için çok mutlu. Bu anlamda da ben Bakanlığımıza teşekkürlerimi sunuyorum.
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Denizli'de hiç sorun yok mu bu konularda?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Evet, Denizli de...
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Hiç bir sorun yok mu bu konularda?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Ne konuda?
ÖMER FETHİ GÜRER (Niğde) - Engellilerle ilgili hiç mi sana şikâyet gelmedi? Bak daha şimdi telefon çaldı ya.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Hiç merak etmeyin.
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Sayın Gürer, lütfen, siz süreniz geldiğinde Niğde'nin sorunlarından bahsedin.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Sayın Bakanım, ben 2026 yılınızın hayırlı ve uğurlu olmasını temenni ediyorum. Daha konuşacağımız birçok konu vardı ama sürem yetmediği için o konulara değinemedim. İnşallah, bu toplumun her kesimini öncelikleyen, herkese dokunan Bakanlığımızın, Bakanlığımız bütçesinin bereketlenmesini temenni ediyorum.
Teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Thank you, Mr. President.
Dear Minister, esteemed members of our esteemed Commission, Deputy Ministers, distinguished representatives of our esteemed press; I greet you all with love and respect.
Unfortunately, I offer my condolences to our soldiers who died and were martyred in our plane crash in Georgia yesterday. May they rest in peace. I offer my condolences to our families and our country.
Of course, Mr. Minister, first of all, I would like to thank you very much for your presentation, it was really very detailed, every area was tried to be touched upon. In fact, it is one of the most important Ministries of our country and a Ministry that provides services to both the most important Ministry and the most important vulnerable segments of the country. In this sense, I congratulate all of you and thank you for all the work you have done.
Of course, we say that we have been serving for twenty-three years with the understanding of strong family, strong Turkey, with a family-oriented perspective, but of course, when we say strong family, we also attach great importance to strong women because we are aware that if the woman is not strong, the family will not be strong, and if the family is not strong, the country will not be strong. Yes, in fact, we think that women's and family policies are two important policies that complement and support each other and strengthen the foundations of society together, and we produce policies intensively, that is, we never see women's rights and family policies as opposing each other, we never did, on the contrary, we see them as complementary, we carry out very serious work towards the empowerment of both women and the family.
Now, when women's rights are mentioned, the first thing that comes to mind is undoubtedly education because education is the most important equal opportunity for women to exercise their economic, social and cultural rights. When women's access to education is denied, not only their future but also the development potential of the entire society is hindered. In the past, women who could not freely participate in education were prevented from education and the labor market, and social progress was actually hindered by the so-called imposition of the acceptable woman, but as you know, with our struggle, we eliminated inequalities in education, we buried the bans in history and implemented policies to ensure that women take more part in education. We think this is a very important gain not only for women, but also for society as a whole. Of course, there have been criticisms about women's participation rates in the workforce, but if women in the past had been able to continue their education fully or take a more active role in their business lives because of their appearance in that job, perhaps these rates would have been in much different places. But today, our female students have filled that gap among university students; It has increased to 52 percent, again, when we look at the rate of female judges, it is around 47 percent, when we look at the rate of teachers in the public sector, it is around 60 percent, and our rate of female entrepreneurs is around 36 percent.

You know, of course, the republic
We achieved significant gains in the early years, thanks to Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, with the right of Turkish women to vote and be elected in Parliament. Of course, in those years, the rate was around 4 percent, in the following years this rate decreased significantly, but until 2002, it had not exceeded that 4 percent threshold, but thank God, at the point we are today, yes, is it very good? It is not, but it has decreased from 4 percent since 2002 and has increased to 20 percent today. Of course, we, as the AK PARTY, made very important promises to women in our election manifesto during our first governments, especially regarding violence against women, custom and honor killings, and today we are actually happy to have fulfilled these promises. With the constitutional amendment in 2004, "Women and men are equal." We kept our promise by including this provision in the text of the Constitution.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - You left the Istanbul Convention, what else are you saying?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Again, with the radical changes we made in the Turkish Penal Code in 2005, violence and sexual assault against women were defined as crimes for the first time and we took effective legal measures against all kinds of discrimination against women. Now, today, "What are you doing for women?" It was said, in fact, even these are very important, the steps we have taken in this direction.
Again, you know, there are ASDEPs about family, I want to touch on the subject of family. It is our most valuable social institution for us, I attach great importance to this institution, Mr. Minister.
SÜMEYYE BOZ (Muş) - There are injustices in ASDEP, there is also salary injustice.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - To date, they have been able to enter more than 10 million households and they have the chance to produce on-site solutions and observe the family on-site. Frankly, we have 5,614 ASDEP employees and have reached more than 10 million households; I think this is very important.
Again, we support our youth by encouraging them regarding the Family and Youth Fund. We provide support of up to 150 thousand lira, with a two-year grace period, 48-month maturity and interest-free, to young people who will establish a home in the fund. Of course, this will increase to 250 thousand liras in 2026, and so far nearly 60 thousand couples have benefited from these loan opportunities.
Regarding maternity benefits, yes, the figures were very low, but together we achieved a significant increase here in the Planning and Budget Commission. I thank you very much in this sense. Of course, when we look at it, it is debatable whether it is enough or not, but I think it is very important that 5 thousand monthly payments for the first child, 1,500 monthly payments for the second child and 5 thousand monthly payments for the third child and that this continues until the children reach the age of five, we have implemented an aid payment of 5.86 billion, almost 6 billion, for 567,546 children in the May-October period alone.
Yes, combating violence against women; When we say the principle of zero tolerance in the fight against violence against women, "You are not doing anything about it." It was said.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - Then why do we rank first in OECD countries with 38 percent?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - No, on the contrary, we have implemented so many policies regarding this, as I just said, as in the Turkish Penal Code.

So, when we look at it this way, before 2002, the issue of violence against women could not even be put into a systematic policy framework, which was often ignored. When we look at what is happening in the world, contrary to what those who instrumentalize the fight against violence against women say, Türkiye has passed its own independent law on the fight against violence against women, namely 6284. Yes, "Istanbul Convention" was said, "You left the Istanbul Convention. Women were deemed null and void in one night." It was said. Yes, the Istanbul Convention has been withdrawn. We came out of the Istanbul Convention with marginal groups and different dimensions, but this is about domestic law because we came out of it.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - What does it have to do with it, where are you drawing the issue?
ALIYE TEAM ERSEVER (Ankara) - What does it have to do with it?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - I'm about this now.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - Where do we rank among OCED countries? We are in first place.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Ms. Asu, as follows: Look, today we are the budget of the Ministry of Family and we are mainly women, I listened to you respectfully. Frankly, I think that we, as women here today, in this Commission, should listen to each other's speeches with respect and then reveal our differences.
REŞAT KARAGÖZ (Amasya) - Yes, you should respect the dead.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Now, I don't understand what you objected to about my statement, but now we have left the Istanbul Convention, but look, the Council of Europe on Violence Against Women dated 24 May 2024.
It has published the Struggle Directive, and when we compare it with Türkiye and the Istanbul Convention, which 45 countries have signed, the directive actually says: "You have signed this agreement, and unfortunately, the countries in the European Union, you have not made the necessary arrangements regarding it." The European Parliament says: "To complete this directive and the 51-article directive, you complete them, and you have been given three years to complete this." When we look at Türkiye, it is the only and exemplary country that has implemented the regulations regarding all the articles in the directive in both the Law No. 6284 and the Turkish Penal Code and domestic legal regulations. As I said before, we defined violence against women as a crime for the first time with the Turkish Penal Code regulation we made in 2005, long before the signing of the contract in 2011.
Now, just because we left the Istanbul Convention does not mean that we have given up on a struggle. This is actually ignoring the work done so far. Turkey continued to implement regulations in this direction even after leaving the agreement. "What are these?" If we say so, regulations such as making violence against women a catalog crime, punishing the crime committed against a divorced spouse as if it were committed against a married spouse, and removing the good behavior discount are the regulations that we later implemented and implemented together in the Parliament. So much so that our country is currently implementing measures such as changing the identity and address of the victim and applying electronic handcuffs, which are not even foreseen in the directive.

Today, thanks to Law No. 6284, amendments to the Turkish Penal Code, Constitutional regulations, the principle of equality in the Civil Code and most importantly, our national action plans, it is an indication that we have become data-based and continuous in this struggle, rather than institutional. Yes, we are now waging a strategic, not periodic, struggle in this field with national action plans that have been prepared regularly since 2007. There is an electronic handcuff application in 81 provinces, and approximately 1,200 criminals with electronic handcuffs are monitored daily. We have implemented important regulations such as KADES and Alo 183 Violence Line.
As for women's guesthouses, as of September 2025, 150 women's shelters are in service, and the number of those affiliated only with our Ministry is 112; Its total capacity is 2,819. Today, 19 guesthouses in 16 provinces provide services according to the special needs of the victims. Criticisms were made about the reduction in the number of guesthouses. We all know that municipalities with a population of over 100 thousand are obliged to open guesthouses. Now, when we look at something like this, when we look at the municipalities - I looked at Izmir - when we look at Istanbul, for example, the Metropolitan Municipality in Istanbul established one, it was established in 2014, during the AK Party period in 2014, there is nothing additional. Moreover, we need to look at the capacity of these houses. In your opinion, I know it is not a question-answer method, but what do you think is the capacity of women's guesthouses today, and what is their occupancy rate? 69 percent, meaning women's shelters should of course be established within the scope of need. In fact, I wish municipalities would fulfill their duties, but when we look at it. (Noise from CHP benches)
SEMRA DİNÇER (Ankara) - He is obsessed with municipalities, let the Ministry set up! Do not cut the municipality's resources!
AYŞE KEŞİR (Düzce) - There is the law, the law.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - If you do not cut the resources of the municipality. You are cutting off the municipality's resources.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Cavit, please mention it in your speech.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Look, so far there is no such thing as cutting off the resources of the municipality. (Noise from CHP benches) In Türkiye, the resources given to the municipality and the resources provided by the Bank of Provinces, the money and amount per capita is certain, regardless of all party members in Türkiye. Municipalities will make choices here, what are their priorities? (Noise from CHP benches)
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Let's close the Ministry and give it to the municipalities! The Ministry is not doing business!
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Since we are so sensitive, we need to look at it first.
Let's come to Izmir, there are 6 women's guesthouses in Izmir, 4 of these 6 have been closed by the municipalities. Now, when we look at it, I think we need to go and ask your municipalities about these, "Why are you closing them?" saying.
Dear Minister, I find it very important; As you know, there was no national database on violence against women in 2002, it was done in 2008, then in 2014, ten years ago, and finally in 2024 and 2025, a Research on Violence Against Women in Türkiye was conducted in cooperation with TÜİK and Marmara University and another university.

Clearly, we see that the research was designed to ensure national representativeness with its wide-ranging samples. Now, the latest research is as follows; In face-to-face interviews with 18,275 women, the following emerged: As a result of this research, it was determined that 28.2 percent of women were subjected to psychological violence, 18.3 percent to economic violence, and 12.8 percent to physical violence throughout their lives; Again, in the last twelve months, it was revealed that 11.6 percent of women were subjected to psychological violence, 3.7 percent to digital violence, 3.2 percent to economic violence, 3.1 percent to persistent stalking, and 2.6 percent to physical violence. Of course, when we look at it this way, according to the "Prevalence Estimates of Violence Against Women" report published by the World Health Organization in 2021, we see that the world average is 27 percent, meaning that violence against women is an issue that not only Türkiye but also the whole world needs to seriously fight against.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - You cannot get away like this, we are 1st in the OECD, 38 percent.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Look, Ms. Asu, we cannot get away by saying this, we have demonstrated what we have done so far.
Now, when we look at the research conducted by the European Union Fundamental Rights Agency, we see that, according to 2024 data, an average of 13.5 percent of women in European countries are exposed to physical violence; Ours is actually at the same rate as the European Union. Violence, of course.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - 38 percent, not the same; We are 1st, 1st.
AYŞE KEŞİR (Düzce) - They don't have any data. You are talking ignorantly.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - OECD data is not like that, Ms. Ayşe, please.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Look, when we look at the figures right now, it is as follows: When we look at the average of femicide per 100 thousand female population, it is 1.27 percent in the world; For example, in America it is 3 percent, now in Türkiye this rate is 0.66. Not even a single hair of a woman should be harmed, she should not be killed, etc., but "You are not doing anything. Sir, huge murders, every day." I think this is unfair. We all need to fight this collectively; Regardless of party, your party, our party, we all need to fight together.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - You are in the executive authority, you are the one who runs the state, you are in the executive authority; Just being sad is not enough.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - I want to say this here: I want to express here that you are being unfair to us.
ALIYE TIMİSİ ERSEVER (Ankara) - You are constantly making a reduction in impunity.
AYŞE KEŞİR (Düzce) - We removed them, we removed them, you couldn't follow them.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Again, this research we have conducted is a study that covers digital violence and persistent stalking for the first time. I think it is an important database that will reveal our state's level of awareness against the new risks brought by technology. Dear Minister, I congratulate you again for your sensitivity on this issue.
"Kadingirisimci.gov.tr" I would like to say here, I think it is a very nice page. Women entrepreneurs and cooperatives can sell the products they produce here; I think we need to publicize this more.

Now, there has been a significant increase in the number of women's cooperatives with this KOOP-DES support. My time is running out.
Of course, as you know, we have seen the social state as a state responsibility, not a choice, since 2002. With this understanding, we have never actually seen social assistance as a blessing, but I honestly think that we have made it a right. Our story is a story of development where a strong state and compassionate hands join hands. Today, we are one of the countries with the best functioning and most comprehensive social support system in the world. We have allocated 917 billion TL for these supports in 2026, this amount corresponds to 4.8 percent of our budget.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - You count all the aid you provide to empower women as women. You mention birth aid to women, and you say aid to families is given to women.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - We are increasing the ratio of social aid and supports to gross domestic product, which was 0.4 percent in 2002, to 1.2 percent in 2026.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - But 1 percent for women's empowerment.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Ms. Asu, I gave the numbers, I gave what we did, still why. I am not here to satisfy you by shouting on this issue, I want to make my speech, with your permission.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - My concern is not to be satisfied, but whether you are satisfied is what matters.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Again, in order to cover the health premium expenses of our citizens who do not have the power to pay from our 2026 budget, see,
157 billion TL; 106 billion TL within the scope of pensions given to our elderly over 65 years of age, disabled citizens in need of care and their relatives. Look, we implemented Türkiye's first Disability Law in 2005. We increased the quotas for disabled people to be more visible in public; Again, we implemented employer premium supports. Again, we allocate 100 billion lira from this budget to support social housing financing. Again, we have allocated 90 billion TL for home care support for our disabled citizens, 56 billion for education support for our disabled citizens, 44 billion for maternity assistance payments, 23 billion for socioeconomic support payments that enable our children to grow up with their families for economic poverty, and 3 billion TL for foster family practices. Again, we have allocated 486 billion TL to 3.5 million households within the scope of electricity production support, and 117 billion TL to 679 thousand households for natural gas support. Thus, we have allocated resources in this budget to the "Protection and Strengthening of the Family" program with an increase of over 30 percent compared to the previous year, the "Protection and Development of Children" program with an increase of 34 percent, and the "Empowerment of Women" program with an increase of 35 percent compared to the previous year.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Two minutes additional time.
ASU KAYA (Osmaniye) - 1.2 percent.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - I am summarizing, Mr. President.
I know, my time is running out, but this is something I find very important, especially regarding children's homes, and I attach great importance to the child growing up in a family environment. I visit children's homes from time to time, our children there only stay in a family environment, before they stayed in ward-type places.

Now, by staying there, our children not only get a family environment, but each of our children go to different areas of interest, such as art, music, sports, and they are provided with private lessons, and they are incredibly happy. We are very happy to see our children's faces smiling. In this sense, I would like to thank our Ministry.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Are there any problems in Denizli regarding these issues?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Yes, in Denizli.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Is there any problem in these matters?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - About what?
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - Have you ever received any complaints about disabled people? Look, the phone just rang.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Don't worry.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Gürer, please talk about Niğde's problems when your time comes.
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Dear Minister, I hope that your year 2026 will be auspicious and auspicious. We had many more topics to talk about, but I couldn't touch on those topics because I didn't have enough time. I hope that our Ministry, which prioritizes every segment of this society and touches everyone, and our Ministry's budget will be blessed.
Thank you.

records.originalTurkish:
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Teşekkür ediyorum Sayın Başkan.
Çok Değerli Bakanım, çok Değerli Komisyonumuzun kıymetli üyeleri, Bakan Yardımcılarım, çok değerli basınımızın güzide temsilcileri; ben de hepinizi sevgi ve saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Maalesef, dün Gürcistan'da düşen uçağımızda vefat eden, şehit olan askerlerimize başsağlığı diliyorum. Mekânları cennet olsun. Ailelerimizin, vatanımızın başı sağ olsun diyorum.
Tabii, Sayın Bakanım, öncelikle sunumunuz için çok teşekkür ediyorum, gerçekten çok detaylı, her alana dokunulmaya çalışılmış. Aslında ülkemizin de en önemli Bakanlıklarından biri ve ülkenin hem en önemli Bakanlığı hem de en önemli hassas kesimlerine hizmet götüren bir Bakanlık. Bu anlamda da sizin şahsınızda tüm götürmüş olduğunuz çalışmalardan dolayı hepinizi tebrik ediyorum, teşekkürlerimi sunuyorum.
Tabii, biz diyoruz ki güçlü aile, güçlü Türkiye anlayışıyla, aile odaklı bir bakış açısıyla bugüne kadar, yirmi üç yıldır hizmet götürdük ama tabii ki güçlü aile derken güçlü kadını da çok büyük önemsiyoruz çünkü eğer kadın güçlü olmazsa aile güçlü olmaz, aile güçlü olmazsa da ülkenin güçlü olmayacağının bilincinin farkındayız. Evet, aslında biz kadın ve aile politikalarını birbirini tamamlayan ve destekleyen, el birliğiyle toplumun temellerini sağlamlaştıran 2 önemli politika olduğunu düşünüyoruz ve bu yoğun da politikalar üretiyoruz yani kadın hakları ile aile politikalarını hiçbir zaman birbirinin karşıtı olarak görmüyoruz, asla da görmedik, tam tersini bunu bir tamamlayıcı olarak görüyoruz, hem kadınların hem de ailenin güçlenmesi yönünde çok ciddi çalışmalar yapıyoruz.
Şimdi, kadın hakları denildiğinde akla ilk gelen kuşkusuz ki eğitimdir çünkü eğitim, kadınların ekonomik, sosyal ve kültürel haklarını kullanabilmesi için en önemli fırsat eşitliğidir. Kadınların eğitime erişimi engellendiğinde sadece onların geleceği değil, tüm toplumun kalkınma potansiyeli de engellenmiş olur. Geçmişte özgürce eğitime katılamayan kadınların eğitim ve iş gücü piyasasından da engellenerek sözde hani, o makbul kadın dayatmasıyla toplumsal ilerlemenin önüne aslında set çekilmişti ama biliyorsunuz, yapmış olduğumuz mücadelemizle eğitimdeki eşitsizlikleri ortadan kaldırdık, yasakları tarihe gömerek kadınların eğitimde daha fazla yer almasını sağlamak yönünde politikaları hayata geçirdik. Bunun sadece kadınlar için değil, aynı zamanda toplumun tümü için çok önemli bir kazanç olduğunu düşünüyoruz. Tabii ki kadının iş gücüne katılım oranları konusunda eleştiriler oldu ama işte o geçmişte kadınlar tam anlamıyla eğitim hayatlarına devam edebilseydi ya da iş hayatlarına o işte görünüşlerinden dolayı iş hayatlarında da aktif olarak daha fazla rol alabilselerdi belki bu oranlar çok daha farklı yerlerde olabilirdi. Ama bugün üniversite öğrencileri arasında kız öğrencilerimiz o eksikliği, farkı kapatmış durumda; yüzde 52'ye çıkmış durumda, yine, kadın hâkim oranına baktığımızda yüzde 47'lerde, yine kamudaki öğretmen oranına baktığımızda yüzde 60 civarında, kadın girişimci oranımız da yüzde 36'larda.
Biliyorsunuz, tabii, cumhuriyetin
ilk yıllarında önemli kazanım elde ettik Mustafa Kemal Atatürk sayesinde, Türk kadınının Parlamentoda seçme ve seçilme hakkıyla birlikte. Değildir ama 2002'den itibaren yüzde 4 kırılarak bugün yüzde 20'lere çıkmış durumda. Tabii, biz AK PARTİ olarak ve ilk hükûmetlerimiz zamanında seçim beyannamemizde de özellikle kadına yönelik şiddet, töre ve namus cinayetleriyle ilgili kadınlara çok önemli sözler vermiştik ve bugün bu sözleri yerine getirmiş olmanın da aslında bir mutluluğunu yaşıyoruz. 2004 yılında Anayasa değişikliğiyle "Kadın ve erkek eşittir." hükmünü Anayasa metnine dâhil ederek bu sözümüzü tuttuk.

Mücadele Direktifi'ni yayınlamış durumda ve bununla biz aslında Türkiye'yi böyle kıyasladığımızda ve 45 ülkenin imza koyduğu İstanbul Sözleşmesi'yle aslında direktif diyor ki: "Siz bu sözleşmeye imza koydunuz ve Avrupa Birliğindeki ülkeler maalesef, siz bununla ilgili gerekli düzenlemeleri yapmadınız." diyor yani diyor ki Avrupa Parlamentosu: "Bu direktifi tamamlamak için ve 51 maddelik direktifi, siz bunları tamamlayın, bunu tamamlamanız için de üç yıllık size süre tanınmıştır." Türkiye'ye baktığımızda söz konusu direktifteki maddelerin tamamıyla ilgili düzenlemeleri gerek 6284 sayılı Kanun'da gerekse Türk Ceza Kanunu'nu ve iç hukuki düzenlemeleri hayata geçirmiş tek ve örnek ülkedir ve sözleşmeye imza konulan 2011'den çok çok daha önce 2005 yılında yaptığımız Türk Ceza Kanunu düzenlemesiyle kadına yönelik şiddeti biraz önce de söylediğim gibi ilk defa suç olarak tanımladık.
Şimdi, İstanbul Sözleşmesi'nden çıkmış olmamız demek bir mücadeleden vazgeçmişiz anlamında değildir. Bu aslında, bugüne kadar yapılan çalışmaların görmezden gelinmesidir. Türkiye, gerçekten sözleşmeden çıktıktan sonra da bu yönde düzenlemeleri hayata geçirmeye devam etmiştir. "Bunlar nelerdir?" dersek, kadına yönelik şiddetin katalog suç hâline getirilmesi, boşanılmış eşe karşı işlenen suçun tıpkı nikâhlı eşe işlenmiş gibi ceza alması, iyi hâl indiriminin kaldırılması gibi düzenlemeler, daha sonra hayata geçirdiğimiz, hep beraber Mecliste hayata geçirdiğimiz düzenlemelerdir. Öyle ki direktifte dahi öngörülmeyen mağdurun kimliğinin ve adresinin değiştirilmesi, elektronik kelepçe uygulaması gibi tedbirleri de hâlihazırda ülkemiz uygulamaktadır.
Bugün 6284 sayılı Kanun, Türk Ceza Kanunu'ndaki değişiklikler, Anayasa düzenlemeleri, Medeni Kanun'daki eşitlik ilkesi ve en önemlisi ulusal eylem planlarımız sayesinde aslında bu mücadelede artık kurumsal değil veriye dayalı ve sürekli hâle geldiğimizin de göstergesidir. Evet, 2007 yılından bu yana düzenli olarak hazırlanan ulusal eylem planlarıyla artık bu alanda dönemsel değil stratejik bir mücadele yürütüyoruz. 81 ilde elektronik kelepçe uygulaması var ve yine yaklaşık günlük 1.200 elektronik kelepçe takılan suçlular takip edilmekte. Yine KADES, yine Alo 183 Şiddet Hattı gibi önemli düzenlemeleri hayata geçirdik.

Sayın Bakanım, çok önemli bulduğum; biliyorsunuz, 2002 yılında kadına yönelik şiddete dair ulusal bir veri tabanı yoktu, bununla ilgili 2008 yılında yapılmıştı, sonra 2014 yılında, on sene önce, en son da işte 2024 ve 2025 dönemlerinde TÜİK'le yine Marmara Üniversitesinde ve diğer üniversitenin iş birliği içinde Türkiye'de Kadına Yönelik Şiddet Araştırması yapıldı. Açıkçası, araştırmanın geniş kapsamlı örnekleriyle ulusal temsiliyeti sağlayacak biçimde tasarlanmış olduğunu görüyoruz. Şimdi, son araştırma da şöyle; 18.275 kadınla yüz yüze yapılan görüşmelerde şu çıkmış: Kadınların yüzde 28,2'sinin yaşamı boyunca psikolojik, yüzde 18,3'ünün ekonomik, yüzde 12,8'inin fiziksel şiddete maruz kaldığı bu araştırma sonucunda belirlenmiş; yine, son on iki ayda ise kadınların yüzde 11,6'sının psikolojik, yüzde 3,7'sinin dijital, yüzde 3,2'sinin ekonomik, yüzde 3,1'inin ısrarlı takip, yüzde 2,6'sının fiziksel şiddete maruz kaldığı ortaya çıkmıştır. Tabii, şöyle baktığımızda, Dünya Sağlık Örgütünün de 2021 tarihli son yayınladığı "Kadına Yönelik Şiddet Yaygınlık Tahminleri" raporuna göre dünya ortalamasının yüzde 27 olduğunu görüyoruz yani kadına yönelik şiddet sadece Türkiye'nin değil tüm dünyanın ciddi anlamda mücadele etmesi gereken bir konudur.
